# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

export const config = {
  runtime: 'edge',
};

interface StreamRequestBody {
  content: string;
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
}

export default async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  if (request.method !== 'POST') {
    return new Response('Method Not Allowed', { status: 405, headers: { Allow: 'POST' } });
  }

  try {
    const { content, headers: userProvidedHeaders } = (await request.json()) as StreamRequestBody;

    if (typeof content !== 'string') {
      return new Response('Bad Request: "content" must be a string in the request body.', { status: 400 });
    }

    const encoder = new TextEncoder();
    const stream = new ReadableStream({
      start(controller) {
        // Enqueue the entire content string as a single chunk
        controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(content));
        controller.close(); // Signal that the stream is complete
      },
    });

    const responseHeaders = new Headers(userProvidedHeaders);

    // Ensure a Content-Type is set for the streamed data, defaulting to text/plain
    if (!responseHeaders.has('Content-Type')) {
      responseHeaders.set('Content-Type', 'text/plain; charset=utf-8');
    }

    // Vercel Edge runtime automatically handles Transfer-Encoding: chunked for streaming responses.
    // Explicitly setting Content-Length might prevent streaming in some cases, so avoid it for streams.

    return new Response(stream, {
      status: 200,
      headers: responseHeaders,
    });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error processing streaming request:', error);

    // Provide a more specific error for JSON parsing issues
    if (error instanceof SyntaxError) {
      return new Response(`Bad Request: Invalid JSON in request body. ${error.message}`, { status: 400 });
    }

    return new Response(`Internal Server Error: ${(error as Error).message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}
